the SUMMATOR are nearly equidistant in a logarithmic scale. This is backed indeed by Figure 6.1, where the nodes for a summation up to  $ s_{\max} = 10^7 $ are shown for required tolerances  $ \text{tol} = 10^{-2} $ and  $ \text{tol} = 10^{-4} $. When  $ s_{\max} $ is increased to higher values, the distribution of the nodes remains qualitatively the same. The reached true error and the number of chosen nodes are presented in Table 6.1 for different tolerances. The exact value of the sum with  $ s_{\max} = 10^7 $ has been computed on a SPARC station  $ 1 + (90 \text{ sec CPU time}) $. The computing time of the SUMMATOR has been maximal 1 sec (CPU) for the most time-consuming test (2140 nodes).


<table border=1 style='margin: auto; word-wrap: break-word;'><tr><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>tol</td><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>error</td><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>nodes</td></tr><tr><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>$ 10^{-2} $</td><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>$ 1 \cdot 10^{-3} $</td><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>47</td></tr><tr><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>$ 10^{-4} $</td><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>$ 5 \cdot 10^{-5} $</td><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>129</td></tr><tr><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>$ 10^{-6} $</td><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>$ 6 \cdot 10^{-7} $</td><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>356</td></tr><tr><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>$ 10^{-8} $</td><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>$ 2 \cdot 10^{-10} $</td><td style='text-align: center; word-wrap: break-word;'>2140</td></tr></table>

<div style="text-align: center;">TABLE 6.1: Performance of the SUMMATOR for the harmonic series.</div>


<table border=1 style='margin: auto; width: max-content;'>
  <thead><tr><th style='text-align: center;'>s</th><th style='text-align: center;'>Value</th></tr></thead>
  <tbody>
    <tr><td style='text-align: center;'>1</td><td style='text-align: center;'></td></tr>
  </tbody>
</table>

<div style="text-align: center;">FIGURE 6.1: Grids for the summation of the harmonic series in a logarithmic scale. Coarse grid Tol = 10⁻² (47 nodes), fine grid TOL = 10⁻⁴ (129 nodes).</div>


Example 6.2. The solution of CODE's by means of the discrete Galerkin method requires the evaluation of scalar products like  $ (Au, \psi_n)_{\rho,\alpha} $, with A an operator from a CODE and  $ \psi_n $ a basis function of  $ H_{\rho,\alpha} $. Whenever an analytic calculation is not possible, the scalar products have to be computed numerically.
